This got changed to:

So, they modified the condition by changing the comparison from less than 0 to less than 1 for the first part.

Hmm, so why did some tests pass and others fail? The key here is understanding what the condition does in the code. 

The condition checks if ( instantLocal XOR instantUTC ) is less than 0 in the original code. Changing it to less than 1 might change how overflow is detected. If this condition triggers an exception, changing it could affect when that exception is thrown.

In TestMonthDay_Properties, maybe the mutation fixed a bug where the month name was incorrect (September vs September). That test started passing, so perhaps the change resolved some issue in how months were being handled or calculated elsewhere.

But for the BuddhistChronology test, which deals with duration fields, this mutation might have introduced an error. Maybe now certain durations aren't being computed correctly because the condition isn't triggering when it should, leading to incorrect values and thus failing tests.

I'm not entirely sure about the specifics of each test, but I can infer that changing the condition affects overflow detection in DateTimeZone calculations, which could ripple into other parts of date/time handling. 

So, putting this together, the mutant change did cause some tests to pass (fixing an issue) and others to fail (introducing new issues). This indicates that there's a fault adjacent effect because the mutation introduced changes that affected multiple areas beyond just fixing one test.
